title: Microsorum sarawakense
taxonomy: Microsorum sarawakense is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern that is native to Borneo. Microsorum sarawakense is a member of the genus Microsorum, which comprises various species of terrestrial ferns. The classification of Microsorum sarawakense places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.

intro: Microsorum sarawakense, also known as Sarawak microsorum or Water Clover, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and swelling, and preventing infection. The bioactive constituents of Microsorum sarawakense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
